summary: Import-time credential exfiltration and reverse shell to 2.25.186.116
details: @variational/common-ui@1.0.9 advertises itself as a shared UI constants package but lib/index.js executes attack code on every require(). A setTimeout-scheduled routine scrapes process.env for keys matching /KEY|SECR|TOK|PASS|PRIV|MNEM|AWS|FIRE|DB_|PG|SIGN|VAULT|SEED|DEPLOY/i, gathers hostname, username, cwd, output of `id`/`whoami`, and the contents of /proc/1/environ, and POSTs the collected data as JSON to http://2.25.186.116:80/t. The same routine opens a TCP socket to 2.25.186.116:443, spawns /bin/sh, and pipes the shell's stdin/stdout/stderr through the socket — an interactive reverse shell on any machine that imports the module. Module names ('http', 'child_process', 'net', 'os'), the destination IP, and the '/bin/sh' path are constructed via numeric arrays passed through String.fromCharCode to evade literal-string review. Any developer, CI runner, or build system that installs and loads this package is fully compromised: cloud, database, signing, mnemonic, and deployment secrets are exfiltrated, and the attacker obtains shell access to the host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
